ops.title: exec lane drops ErrPathNotFound identity (exit 1 instead of 3)
ops.description: Found by the fail-fast-read refuter pass: internal/codeexec/wire.go serializes host-op failures as err.Error() strings into the monty subprocess, so backend.ErrPathNotFound loses errors.Is identity and `ccx exec '... await read("/nonexistent/x", full=True) ...'` exits 1 while the direct CLI read exits 3. Message is correct; only the exit-code taxonomy leaks. Fix needs structured error codes across the sandbox boundary (string-matching errors is banned by STYLEGUIDE).

ops.text: exec-lane host-op failures carry a structured category across the monty boundary so ExitCode maps ErrPathNotFound-rooted failures to exit 3
